name: La Bobia and Sierra de Penouta Loop
name_original: Desde El Folgueirou a la Sierra de San Isidro — circular por Vegadeo
description: Tackle the difficult La Bobia and Sierra de Penouta Loop, a 33.3-mile road cycling route with 4809 feet of elevation gain in the Oscos-Eo Bi
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Illano traverse a diverse and rugged landscape in Asturias, Spain. The region features mountainous terrain with significant elevation changes, offering challenging climbs and winding roads. Cyclists encounter a mix of valleys, rivers, and forests, providing varied experiences within an unspoiled natural environment. This topography is ideal for road cyclists seeking demanding routes with rewarding views. Best road cycling routes around Illano The most popular road cycling route is Oscos, Navia Basin, and Sierra de Penouta Route, a 33.2 miles (53.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 16 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Bobia and Sierra de Penouta Loop, a difficult 33.3 miles (53.6 km) path. This loop offers challenging climbs and descents across the Sierra de Penouta. Local road cyclists also love the Alto de Penouta – La Bobia Ridge loop from Mirador de Penouta Interior, a 59.0 miles (94.9 km) trail leading through high ridges and offering panoramic viewpoints, often completed in about 5 hours 35 minutes. Road cycling around Illano is defined by mountainous terrain, winding roads, and river valleys. The network offers options primarily for experienced cyclists seeking challenging routes. The routes in Illano are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 90 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Illano's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Illano?
A: Illano offers a selection of 15 road cycling routes, catering primarily to experienced cyclists seeking challenging terrain.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for road cycling in Illano?
A: Road cycling in Illano is characterized by a diverse and rugged landscape, featuring mountainous terrain, winding roads, and river valleys. You'll encounter significant elevation changes, forests, and an unspoiled natural environment, typical of Asturias.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes in Illano?
A: Yes, Illano is known for its challenging road cycling. Out of the 15 available routes, 9 are classified as difficult, featuring substantial elevation gains and demanding climbs. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077406285/oscos-navia-basin-and-sierra-de-penouta-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oscos, Navia Basin, and Sierra de Penouta Route&lt;/a&gt; covers over 53 km with more than 1,400 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What kind of scenic views can I expect on road cycling routes around Illano?
A: Road cycling routes in Illano offer immersion in an unspoiled landscape with birch, oak, cork oak, and hazelnut trees. You can expect panoramic views from elevated points, especially along routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24576806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Penouta – La Bobia Ridge loop from Mirador de Penouta Interior&lt;/a&gt;. The region is part of 'The Historical Park of the Navia River,' providing stunning scenery between the sea and the mountains.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to see along road cycling routes in Illano?
A: While cycling, you might encounter glimpses of traditional Asturian architecture in villages. Nearby attractions that could be incorporated into a longer trip include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/872539" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Marina de Buspol Chapel&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/872567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grandas de Salime Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7034965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salime Dam&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Illano?
A: The road cycling routes in Illano are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.0 stars. Over 90 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the challenging climbs and rewarding views.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Illano?
A: Yes, several routes in Illano are designed as loops, offering a complete circular experience. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353795558/la-bobia-and-sierra-de-penouta-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Bobia and Sierra de Penouta Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which provides challenging climbs and descents across the Sierra de Penouta.

Q: Is Illano suitable for beginner road cyclists?
A: Illano's road cycling routes are primarily geared towards experienced cyclists due to the mountainous terrain and significant elevation changes. While there are 6 routes classified as moderate, there are no easy routes listed, suggesting it might be challenging for absolute beginners.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Illano?
A: Given the mountainous terrain, the best time for road cycling in Illano is typically during the warmer, drier months, from late spring through early autumn. This avoids the harsher conditions of winter and ensures more pleasant riding temperatures and clearer roads.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in Illano?
A: Illano's road cycling routes are generally demanding, with most classified as difficult or moderate and no easy routes available. This suggests that the area might not be ideal for family-friendly road cycling with young children or those seeking very gentle, flat rides.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Illano?
A: While specific parking areas for cyclists are not detailed, many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24576806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Penouta – La Bobia Ridge loop from Mirador de Penouta Interior&lt;/a&gt;, start from specific points like viewpoints or recreational areas. These locations often provide suitable parking for visitors.

Q: Can I access road cycling routes in Illano using public transport with my bike?
A: Information regarding public transport options in Illano that accommodate bicycles is limited. Given the rural nature of the area, it's advisable to plan your journey by car or check local bus services for their bike policies in advance.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes around Illano?
A: Yes, Illano offers several long-distance road cycling routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24576806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Penouta – La Bobia Ridge loop from Mirador de Penouta Interior&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 94.9 km trail, often taking over 5 hours to complete, providing an extensive ride through high ridges.
tips: El Alto de Penouta is another classic from western Asturias. We can choose between several variants, on its north and west faces to climb up to an altitude of 780 meters above sea level. From the top we can see the coast and the distant town of Tapia de Casariego and/or the spectacular valley of the Navia river.

Lovely ride through the crest of la Bobia with stunning views down different valleys.

Especially recommended when you are riding it downwards, as you'll be still fast even when wind works against you (much better riding against the wind here than in the flat!).
Beautiful picture that we found rolling through the Sierra de San Isidro between Cimadevila and the top of Pozo de la Nieve. A marvel for road bike lovers.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the La Bobia and Sierra de Penouta Loop?
faqs.answer: This challenging road cycling loop typically takes around 3 hours and 17 minutes to complete. However, given its significant elevation gain and varied terrain, individual times may vary based on fitness levels and stops for scenery.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult and is not recommended for beginners. It involves substantial elevation gains (over 1,400 meters) and can include varied terrain, with some segments potentially unpaved or requiring cyclists to push their bikes. It requires very good fitness and experience with challenging road rides.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the La Bobia and Sierra de Penouta Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is primarily mountainous, featuring significant climbs and descents. While much of the route is generally well-paved, be prepared for some segments that might be unpaved and difficult, potentially requiring you to dismount and push your bike. This varied surface adds to the challenge and adventure of the loop.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers stunning mountain vistas and expansive views over valleys. Key highlights include the Alto de Penouta, the scenic La Bobia Ridge, and the picturesque Winding Road in Sierra de San Isidro. These points provide excellent opportunities to appreciate the natural beauty of the region.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the mountainous terrain and potential for varied weather, the best time to cycle this loop is generally during the warmer, drier months, typically late spring through early autumn. This avoids the harsher conditions of winter and early spring, ensuring more pleasant cycling and better road conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in the Oscos-Eo Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: No, there are generally no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling this route within the Oscos-Eo Biosphere Reserve. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles to preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: Is the La Bobia and Sierra de Penouta Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily a road cycling loop, which is generally not ideal for dogs, especially given the distance and elevation. While there are no explicit prohibitions, it's best to consider if the conditions are safe and comfortable for your dog, particularly with traffic and the demanding nature of the ride. If you do bring a dog, ensure it is well-behaved and on a leash when appropriate.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other official trails. Notable sections include parts of the Road Oscos, Cuenca del Navia y Sierra de Penouta, and segments of the [CIMA AS32] La Bobia * Puente Urubio / Doiras. You may also encounter shorter portions of the Ruta del puente colgante and various other CIMA AS32 La Bobia routes.

faqs.question: Are there any particularly challenging sections or specific considerations for this road cycling loop?
faqs.answer: Be prepared for significant and sustained climbs, especially towards the Alto de Penouta and along the La Bobia Ridge. Some sections may be unpaved or in rougher condition, which can be challenging for road bikes and might require dismounting. Ensure your bike is in excellent condition and you have appropriate gearing for steep ascents.

faqs.question: Where can I find parking to access this road cycling loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed in the route information, you would typically look for parking in nearby towns or villages that serve as common starting points for routes in the region. It's advisable to check local maps or community information for designated parking facilities near the start of the loop.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: This loop traverses a relatively remote, mountainous area. While there might be small villages or hamlets along the way, dedicated cafes or refreshment stops are not guaranteed. It's highly recommended to carry sufficient water, snacks, and any necessary supplies for the entire duration of your ride.
